title: lithocarpus collettii
intro: Lithocarpus collettii, also known as the Collett's cork oak, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The bark and leaves of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its high tannin content and other bioactive compounds. Lithocarpus collettii can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
